originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc` on line 10 is not freed after the last access at line 17, column 5.

model:
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: MEMORY_LEAK at L17: eckey is allocated at L10 (HAVE_ECC=T) and never freed before return at L24. This is the same known VBDB memory leak as PRODUCT_30 (TRUE, PC=HAVE_ECC). The leak occurs whenever HAVE_ECC=T regardless of SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto — both branches (SSL_CTX=T: 'do something', SSL_CTX=F: '*eckey=15') lead to the same unfreed return. PC adds SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto=F unnecessarily, making it more restrictive than needed. The true condition is just HAVE_ECC=T. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_OVER.
